title: vicia lathyroides subsp. olbiensis
intro: Vicia lathyroides subsp. olbiensis is a subspecies of the vetch plant, also known as the narrow-leaved vetch, which is a flowering plant in the pea family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Vicia lathyroides subsp. olbiensis include its ability to help with urinary issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, it is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be found in some commercial supplements, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
